summary: Pearson correlation and top-10% kappa-vs-target overlap on 7 cases (run_1, 50, 100, 200, 300, 400, 485). Curvature anti-correlates with the dominant WSS_x component (~half of random overlap) and weakly positively correlates with WSS_z. Net |WSS| effect is negative because |WSS_x| dominates magnitude.
interpretation: WSS_x is the dominant component (mean magnitude ~1.5 vs ~0.6 for WSS_y/WSS_z) and is the most strongly anti-correlated with kappa. So the curvature-weighted sampling actively oversamples low-WSS_x regions (smooth panels are good for WSS_x, sharp edges separate flow). WSS_z weakly improves (top-10% capture 13.69% vs random 10%) because vertical shear DOES localize near sharp transverse edges (wheel arches, A-pillar). The advisor's specific watch-list note 'if val_tau_z improves vs baseline at EP3' is the only signal expected to survive.
